label: Chase
description: Update your legal name on your Chase accounts, cards, and records.

process.summary: Visit a Chase branch in person with your updated government-issued ID and legal name change documentation. Chase does not process legal name changes online or by phone.

steps.description: Gather your updated government-issued photo ID (driver's license or passport) and your legal name change documentation (court order).
Visit any Chase branch. Let the associate know you need to update your legal name on your accounts.
steps.note: Branch locations are available at chase.com/branch-atm.

steps.description: The associate updates your name on all accounts. New debit and credit cards will be ordered and mailed. Your account and card numbers remain the same.
Log in to chase.com or the Chase app to confirm the name is updated in your online profile. Update any saved payment methods elsewhere that reference your old name.

access_requirements.travel_note: An in-person branch visit is required.

process.estimated_time_days: Same-day update in branch; 5 to 7 business days for new cards.

discrimination_notes: Chase's non-discrimination policy covers gender identity and expression. The CFPB accepts complaints about discriminatory treatment by financial institutions.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: Same process as most major banks — updated ID plus court order, in person.
